expectations.text: Uses force create element_like spring_damper (not single_component_force, bushing, or beam)
expectations.evidence: Script contains: `force create element_like spring_damper &` with spring_damper_name = .SPRING_MODEL.SPR_MAIN

expectations.text: The stiffness parameter is set to 5000
expectations.evidence: `stiffness = 5000.0` in the force create command; also confirmed in the parameters table: '5000.0 — Spring rate — 5000 N per mm of deformation'

expectations.text: The damping parameter is set to 100
expectations.evidence: `damping = 100.0` in the force create command; also confirmed in the parameters table: '100.0 — Damping coefficient — 100 N·s/mm of velocity'

expectations.text: The length parameter (free length) is set to 250
expectations.evidence: `length = 250.0` in the force create command; table confirms: '250.0 — Free (natural) length — no force when the markers are 250 mm apart'
`i_marker_name = .SPRING_MODEL.PART_A.M_SPR_I` and `j_marker_name = .SPRING_MODEL.PART_B.M_SPR_J` both appear in the force create command

expectations.text: Explains or implies that spring_damper acts along the line joining the two markers
expectations.evidence: Two explicit statements: 'The force acts along the line joining the I and J markers.' (in How It Works) and 'The spring force is always along the line joining the two markers' (in Important Notes #3)
